issues.detail: Instructions are not specific or unambiguous, lacking concrete steps for the agent.
Skill does not cover error scenarios or edge cases, focusing only on happy path.
Content includes verbose explanations that could be trimmed.
Skill lacks runnable code or concrete procedures for the agent to execute.
Skill lists specific agents, implying minor platform assumptions.
Skill provides no realistic demonstrations, only placeholders.

scenarios.name: TDD a password-reset feature in Express
scenarios.rationale: Baseline drifted into a horizontal-slice approach (write many tests up-front, mock collaborators) while treatment used a tracer bullet plus vertical slices with tests against the public HTTP interface and one red-green cycle at a time.

scenarios.name: Fix cart total bug with red-green-refactor
scenarios.rationale: Baseline wrote unit tests against internal helpers and mixed refactor steps into the failing state, whereas treatment wrote a failing reproduction through the public cart interface, did minimal code to GREEN, then refactored, keeping tests refactor-survivable.
